# D(HE)ater

> D(HE)ater is the proof-of-concept implementation of the D(HE)at attack (CVE-2002-20001), a denial-of-service attack that heats up a server CPU by enforcing Diffie-Hellman ephemeral (DHE) key exchange over TLS and SSH. It uses CryptoLyzer to check DHE support of a target service and to generate the traffic required to perform the attack.

D(HE)ater is a command-line tool. The `--protocol` option (`tls` or `ssh`) and the target `uri` are mandatory; `--timeout`, `--thread-num`, and `--key-size` are optional. It requires Python 3.9 or newer.

**Disclaimer:** this is a proof-of-concept denial-of-service tool for defensive testing, mitigation verification, and research only. Use it solely against systems you own or are explicitly authorized to test; unauthorized use may be illegal and will disrupt the target. Provided as-is, without warranty or liability for misuse.

## Documentation

- [README](README.md): overview, requirements, installation, and command-line usage examples
- [Project page](https://dheatattack.com/dheater): background, mitigation guidance, and affected implementations
- [Technical paper](https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/document/10374117): full peer-reviewed description of the D(HE)at attack on IEEE Access

## Reference

- [CVE-2002-20001](https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2002-20001): the vulnerability D(HE)ater demonstrates
- [CryptoLyzer](https://gitlab.com/coroner/cryptolyzer): the dependency used to probe DHE support and generate attack traffic
- [Source repository](https://gitlab.com/dheatattack/dheater): GitLab project hosting the code

## Optional

- [Apache License 2.0](https://choosealicense.com/licenses/apache-2.0/): the license the code is released under
